item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gap is actually them mishearing "Gaap", who is a prince of hell in the Ars Goetia. I assume it's pronounced "Gahp", but I'm not sure. The idea is that the agent fell into Gaap's realm, but instead of running into that particular demon, he meets Stolas, who was merely "visiting" Gaap's realm. I don't know that it's really something a reader would glean from the information as presented, but it just felt more organic for me to write it that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know I probably won’t get an answer, but does the “Gap” thing refer to the Ginungagap from Norse Mythology? Bought a copy of the Prose Edda to read last year since I love mythology. Noting the use of Danish in the text and the other mythological themes e.g. the Ars Goetia, just want clarification. Saw the footnote before reading the transcripts and though is this about norse mythology?

Also, +1 because this is a nice and simple, easy to understand story. Just what is needed sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If somebody doesn't call these demons out on their garbage-y gender essentialism…

item.title: Re: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Thanks for that, appreciated.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As Norwegian is my other native language, I can confirm that these translations are completely accurate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Originally I was on the border of downvoting this, but after letting it percolate for a few days and several rereads, I've decided to upvote. Some parts of this still bug me (it's an aquarium, I would think random splashing has dozens of apparent sources…) but the core interaction and the themes portrayed are compelling. I quite like the idea of skipifying goetic demons as well, being a sucker for mythology as I am.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These comments were so nice to read. I knew this wouldn't work for everyone but it's satisfying to know that I hit the mark as I intended to for some readers. The idea was never to just chuck the Ars Goetia into SCP canon, but to use their depictions as a starting point for the question "What would an encounter with a demon that just looks like a big owl with long legs and a crown be like?" I imagined it would just be sort of incomprehensible and intimidating, rather than outright terrifying or classically "demonic".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mind the Gaap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd have to second this; it hits just the right note between incomprehensible and meaningful enough to dechipher - and it's a nice change of pace to have some nigh-inexplicable weirdness without accompanying exposition. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Translation made that pretty creepy. Well done.

Splashing sounds without any apparent source had been reported in the building for several decades.

Well, I mean, it was an aquarium.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This should be more or less accurate, enough to decipher what's being said. If anyone who is actually fluent in Danish wishes to correct me, that would be swell.

+Translations
– hide block

jeg = I
hans = his
kan = can
du = you
ja = yes
og = and
ikke = not
kvinde = woman
ejendom = property
hjem = home
min = my
spørg = ask
mig = me
dette = this
først = first
evigt = ever

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can someone post a translation of the danish portion of the transcript for those of us not fluent?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jonathan Edwards: "Sinner in the Hands of An Angry, Incomprehensible Strigiform Demon".

Brandt should get the very best psychiatric care. What concerns me is - has he already accomplished Ol' Beaky's assignment, or is some further horror upstream heading down?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You managed to convey an otherworldly presence really well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anything specific or just overall?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like what you have but the audio log is goofy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll ruminate over it a bit. If I wasn't such a folklore nerd it'd have been a fairly easy upvote, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah yeah, that's a fair opinion. Unfortunately I don't know that I can sway you. The inspiration was the artwork for Stolas on the wiki. A long legged owl wearing a crown doesn't really scream terrifying or demonic to me, but I thought it would be interesting to still make it seem alien and otherworldly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm on the fence about this one, as the entity itself seems like a fairly straightforward port of a Goetic demon. It's competently handled, but I dislike existing mythical beings getting shoehorned into the SCPverse in general. No vote.

ETA: My first thought about the entity itself was "Cosmic Owl from Adventure Time". As soon as you mentioned the legs and crown I thought, "Oh, so that's where we're going," and was mildly disappointed, though certainly less so than if the former had been the case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Major thanks to djkaktus for creating the audio clip, his willingness to to follow my weirdly specific instructions for a difficult request was instrumental here. It's been awhile since I received feedback on this, so I don't remember everyone that looked at it originally, but thank you to all that did. Pretty sure that includes Roget and Tuomey, but I'll gladly give credit to anyone I'm forgetting if you remind me. Huge thanks to A Random Day for help with the clinical tone; you're a champ.

I also should mention that this was inspired by artwork and descriptions from the Ars Goetia. The author is anonymous and at least 400 years dead so I don't think I'm in danger of copyright infringement here, though someone can tell me if I'm wrong.
